Verified Petition To Determine Heirship And Entitlement To Estate
Off Calendar Jan-25-2023 Continued To Mar-13-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom:
Brianna Mannion, Esq. for the San Francisco Public Administrator (Administrator).
Evan Winet, Esq. for Russel Patrick Ritchie, Jennifer Christine Martin, Laurinda T. Morrow, Anne Ritchie, Margaret Roseann Ritchie Kaiser, Felicia Bridget Morrow Mesa, Jennifer Ann Morrow, Marcus James Morrow, Theodore Patrick Morrow, Thomas A. Morrow, Deborah Lee Ritchie Penikas, Patricia Louise Ritchie Ripley, Robert Christopher Ritchie, and Carletta Ann Morrow Romanowsky (Heirs-at-Law).

The Court stated that it is concerned with the amount of effort regarding the paternal heir search, directed counsel to page eight of the second amended petition filed in October of 2022, stated that no one can locate the obituaries, and that the problem from the Court's perspective is that there is no incentive from anyone appearing today to look for the paternal heirs because it would reduce the amount going to the maternal heirs. Mr. Winet inquired about the need for additional research. Ms. Mannion inquired as to who Mr. Winet represents, stated that he usually represents the assignees, that if they had found additional heirs they would have come forward, that they had identified who they thought were paternal heirs, that they ran through the names, and that she and her investigator discovered that these individuals were not the line of Thompson. Mr. Winet stated that they represent the American Research Bureau and that they also received authorization from the heirs to represent them in this matter. The Court stated that it needed more information so that it can proceed. Mr. Winet stated that they could do a search and prepare a supplemental declaration for the Court. The Court stated that the supplemental declaration would satisify it and that the declaration should support each date of death.

The Second Amended Verified Petition to Determine Heirship and Entitlement to Estate is continued to March 13, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. Mr. Winet is to file a supplemental declaration.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Cho. Matter not reported.
